name: Division No. 6

actions.title: Citywide residential rezoning for housing (blanket rezoning)
actions.summary: Calgary changed its Land Use Bylaw to make R-CG the default residential district citywide, allowing duplexes, rowhouses, and townhouses. After sustained public opposition (411 speakers, 3,300 written submissions at the repeal hearing), council voted 12-3 to repeal the policy on April 8, 2026. The repeal may jeopardize Calgary's $251 million Housing Accelerator Fund agreement with CMHC.

contextBlurb: Calgary's attempt at blanket residential rezoning became one of Canada's most contentious housing debates. After a 16-day public hearing in 2024, the city enacted the policy, only to repeal it in April 2026 following continued opposition. The reversal puts the city's $251 million federal housing funding at risk.

name: Division No. 11

actions.title: Zoning Bylaw Renewal eliminating single-family-only zoning (Charter Bylaw 20001)
actions.summary: Comprehensive overhaul that eliminated single-family-only zoning. Reduced residential zones from 16 to 6. The small-scale residential zone allows up to 8 units on mid-block sites of 600+ m2. In 2024, the city approved 16,519 new dwelling units, a 30% increase over 2023.

contextBlurb: Edmonton took the boldest step of any major Canadian city, eliminating single-family-only zoning entirely in January 2024. The result: dwelling unit approvals jumped 30% in the first year, with multiplex construction more than doubling. The city has become a national model for zoning reform.

name: Division No. 8
contextBlurb: Red Deer and surrounding communities in Division No. 8 are tracked as part of Alberta housing coverage. The city has seen population growth driven by affordability relative to Calgary and Edmonton.
